---
title: "AD698 - Applied Generative AI"
subtitle: "Project Instructions III - Step 3 (Chunking, Embeddings, and Vector Index)"
number-sections: true
date: "2026-04-28"
date-modified: today
date-format: long
engine: jupyter
execute:
  echo: true
  eval: false
categories: ['M03:', 'Project']
description: "Project guidance for Module 3 focused on Project Instructions III - Step 3 (Chunking, Embeddings, and Vector Index)."
---

# Step 3 Objective

Step 3 builds the **retrieval memory** of your financial technology product — the component that makes it possible to answer questions grounded in specific filings, companies, and time periods within your NAICS industry.

This step is where the product becomes a real search engine over financial disclosures. The quality of your chunking strategy and embedding choices will directly determine whether the product can retrieve relevant evidence for industry-specific questions.

You will:

- chunk cleaned item text with a token-aware strategy
- create chunk-level metadata tied back to NAICS industry, company, year, and item
- generate embeddings via API or open-source model
- build a persistent vector index
- validate top-k retrieval with at least 5 industry-relevant test questions

# Colab Path Setup

```{python}
from pathlib import Path
BASE_DIR = Path('/content/drive/MyDrive/project_sec10k_rag')
# Optional local: BASE_DIR = Path.cwd() / 'project_sec10k_rag'

CLEANED_ITEMS_DIR = BASE_DIR / 'data' / 'cleaned_items'
CHUNKED_DIR = BASE_DIR / 'data' / 'chunked'
EMBEDDINGS_DIR = BASE_DIR / 'data' / 'embeddings'
VECTOR_STORE_DIR = BASE_DIR / 'data' / 'vector_store'

for d in [CHUNKED_DIR, EMBEDDINGS_DIR, VECTOR_STORE_DIR]:
    d.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
```

# Implementation Guidance

## Chunking

Use token-aware chunking with overlap. Keep chunk sizes consistent for stable retrieval.

Minimum metadata per chunk:

- chunk_id
- naics_code
- company_name
- ticker
- filing_year
- item_number
- token_count
- source_file

Including `naics_code` in every chunk allows the product to support multi-industry expansion in the future without re-indexing.

## Embeddings

Allowed options:
- API: OpenAI, Gemini, Claude, Cohere
- Local/open: Mistral, Gemma, BAAI, Qwen via Hugging Face stacks

## Vector Index

Preferred: FAISS
Optional: Chroma

# Retrieval Validation

Run at least 5 test questions and inspect top-k chunk quality manually.

# Required Outputs for Step 3

- data/chunked/sec10k_chunks.parquet
- data/embeddings/
- data/vector_store/

# Submission Checklist (Step 3)

- [ ] Chunking strategy documented
- [ ] Chunk metadata complete (including `naics_code`)
- [ ] Embeddings generated successfully
- [ ] Vector index built and saved
- [ ] Top-k retrieval examples included and reviewed

# Weekly Team Standup

Your team holds a **5-minute standup once per week** during Module 3. Step 3 involves the longest compute times in the project — standups help the team coordinate embedding runs, avoid duplicated work, and flag retrieval quality issues early.

> **Reference:** [Atlassian — How to Run Effective Standups](https://www.atlassian.com/agile/scrum/standups)

Three questions for the team each week:

1. **What did the team complete since the last standup?**
2. **What will the team work on before the next standup?**
3. **What blockers or decisions is the team stuck on?**

## In-Person Sections

Standup happens live at the start of class. Flag embedding API quota issues, FAISS save/load problems, or retrieval quality concerns.

## Online Sections — Weekly Standup Document

Submit a **one-page standup document** to the course portal by the due date each week. Ungraded on its own, but **standup submissions count toward your final project participation grade**.

Use this template:

---

**Team Name / NAICS Industry:**

**Standup Week:** Module 3 — Week ___

**Date Submitted:**

| Question | Team Response |
|---|---|
| What did we complete this week? | |
| What will we work on next week? | |
| What is blocking us or needs a decision? | |

**Files or outputs produced this week** (list filenames or Drive links):

**Team member who facilitated this standup:**

---

:::{.callout-note}
Retrieval quality issues discovered in Step 3 are far easier to fix now than after the RAG generation pipeline is built. Use standup to report retrieval test results and decide whether chunking or embedding parameters need adjustment.
:::
